nN.description: Total number of nodes in the supply network (suppliers, transshipment points, and beneficiary camps)

S.description: Supplier node indices: S[s] is the index in N of supplier s

T.description: Transshipment node indices: T[t] is the index in N of transshipment node t

B.description: Beneficiary camp node indices: B[j] is the index in N of beneficiary camp j

E.description: Adjacency matrix: 1 if a directed edge exists from node i to node j, 0 otherwise

dem.description: Number of beneficiaries at each beneficiary camp

pc.description: Procurement cost per kg of each commodity

tc.description: Transportation cost per kg of commodity k along the edge from node i to node j

nutreq.description: Per-person nutritional requirement for each nutrient

nutval.description: Nutritional value per kg of each commodity for each nutrient

assumptions.description: There is at least one node.
There is at least one supplier node.
There is at least one transshipment node.
There is at least one beneficiary camp.
There is at least one commodity.
There is at least one nutrient.
Each supplier node index is a valid node index (in range [0, nN)).

code.python: assert all(0 <= S[s] < nN for s in range(nS))

assumptions.description: Each transshipment node index is a valid node index (in range [0, nN)).

code.python: assert all(0 <= T[t] < nN for t in range(nT))

assumptions.description: Each beneficiary camp node index is a valid node index (in range [0, nN)).

code.python: assert all(0 <= B[j] < nN for j in range(nB))

assumptions.description: The supplier, transshipment, and beneficiary node-class maps partition N (every node belongs to exactly one class).

code.python: assert set(S) | set(T) | set(B) == set(range(nN)) and set(S).isdisjoint(T) and set(S).isdisjoint(B) and set(T).isdisjoint(B)

assumptions.description: The supplier, transshipment, and beneficiary node-class maps are each injective.

code.python: assert len(set(S)) == nS and len(set(T)) == nT and len(set(B)) == nB

assumptions.description: Number of beneficiaries at each camp is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(dem[j] >= 0 for j in range(nB))

assumptions.description: Procurement cost per kg of each commodity is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(pc[k] >= 0 for k in range(nK))

assumptions.description: Transportation cost along each arc for each commodity is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(tc[i][j][k] >= 0 for i in range(nN) for j in range(nN) for k in range(nK))

assumptions.description: Per-person nutritional requirement for each nutrient is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(nutreq[l] >= 0 for l in range(nL))

assumptions.description: Nutritional value per kg of each commodity for each nutrient is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(nutval[k][l] >= 0 for k in range(nK) for l in range(nL))

F.description: Amount of commodity k shipped from node i to node j (kg)

R.description: Ration size per person of each commodity (kg)

constraints.description: Suppliers are pure sources: no inflow on incoming edges.
constraints.formulation: \sum_{i \in N} E_{i, S_s} F_{i, S_s, k} = 0 \quad \forall s \in N_S, k \in K

constraints.description: Flow conservation at transshipment nodes for each commodity: total inflow equals total outflow (no storage).
constraints.formulation: \sum_{i \in N} E_{ij} F_{ijk} = \sum_{i \in N} E_{ji} F_{jik} \quad \forall j \in N_T, k \in K

constraints.description: Beneficiaries are pure sinks: no outflow on outgoing edges.
constraints.formulation: \sum_{j \in N} E_{B_b, j} F_{B_b, j, k} = 0 \quad \forall b \in N_B, k \in K

constraints.description: Each beneficiary camp receives at least its ration demand on incoming edges for each commodity.
constraints.formulation: \sum_{i \in N} E_{i,B_j} F_{i,B_j,k} \geq \mathrm{dem}_j R_k \quad \forall j \in N_B, k \in K

constraints.description: The ration collectively satisfies all per-person nutritional requirements.
Flow support is acyclic per commodity: there exists a rank labeling that strictly increases along positive-flow edges.
constraints.formulation: \forall k \in K, \exists r : N \to \mathbb{N}, \forall i, j \in N : E_{ij} = 1 \land F_{ijk} > 0 \implies r_i < r_j

code.python: # Acyclicity is verified post-solve via topological sort on the positive-flow support.

constraints.description: Flow is supported on graph edges: no flow on non-edges.
constraints.formulation: E_{ij} = 0 \implies F_{ijk} = 0 \quad \forall i,j \in N, k \in K

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(F[i, j, k] == 0 for i in range(nN) for j in range(nN) for k in range(nK) if E[i][j] == 0)

constraints.description: Flow on each arc for each commodity is non-negative.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(F[i, j, k] >= 0 for i in range(nN) for j in range(nN) for k in range(nK))

constraints.description: Ration size per person of each commodity is non-negative.

objective.description: Minimize total procurement cost plus total transportation cost. Procurement cost is charged on the outflow of each commodity leaving supplier nodes.
objective.formulation: \min \sum_{k \in K} \mathrm{pc}_k \left( \sum_{s \in N_S} \sum_{j \in N} E_{S_s, j} F_{S_s, j, k} \right) + \sum_{i \in N} \sum_{j \in N} \sum_{k \in K} \mathrm{tc}_{ijk} F_{ijk}

code.gurobipy:
model.setObjective(
    gp.quicksum(pc[k] * gp.quicksum(E[S[s]][j] * F[S[s], j, k] for s in range(nS) for j in range(nN)) for k in range(nK))
    + gp.quicksum(tc[i][j][k] * F[i, j, k] for i in range(nN) for j in range(nN) for k in range(nK)),
    GRB.MINIMIZE
)
